# Restcalc — Sleep Calculator & Bedtime Planner

> Restcalc is a free, browser-based sleep calculator and bedtime planner. It helps users find the best time to go to bed or wake up by aligning with natural 90-minute sleep cycles. No signup, no backend, 100% client-side. Available in English (default) and 中文 (Chinese, via an in-page toggle).

## What is Restcalc?

Restcalc is an online sleep-planning tool for people who want to wake up feeling refreshed instead of groggy. It is built on a simple, science-backed idea: a healthy night of sleep is organized into cycles of about 90 minutes, and waking up at the boundary between cycles (during light sleep) feels much better than being jolted out of deep sleep.

The site targets general adults who want better sleep, shift workers, students, parents, and travelers dealing with jet lag. It is not medical advice.

## Interactive Tools

Each tool is a standalone page with its own calculator, explanation, and FAQ.

- [Bedtime Calculator](https://restcalc.vercel.app/tools/bedtime-calculator.html): Enter the time you need to wake up. We count back in 90-minute sleep cycles plus ~15 minutes to fall asleep.
- [Wake-up Calculator](https://restcalc.vercel.app/tools/wake-up-calculator.html): Enter when you plan to go to bed. We add sleep cycles to find the best moments to wake up.
- [Sleep Duration Calculator](https://restcalc.vercel.app/tools/sleep-duration-calculator.html): Enter your bedtime and wake time to see total sleep, completed cycles, and a quality rating.
- [Nap Calculator](https://restcalc.vercel.app/tools/nap-calculator.html): Enter the current time to see the best window for a refreshing power nap or a full-cycle nap.
- [Sleep Window Helper](https://restcalc.vercel.app/tools/sleep-window-calculator.html): Choose the time range you are free to sleep. We find the bedtimes that complete full 90-minute cycles and keep you inside your window.
- [Sleep Log & Trend](https://restcalc.vercel.app/tools/sleep-log.html): Log a few nights — just your bedtime, wake time, and how you felt. We turn it into a visual trend and show your 90-minute cycles each night.

## How Sleep Cycles Work

Your brain moves through sleep cycles roughly every 90 minutes, alternating between light sleep, deep sleep, and REM (dream) sleep. A full night usually contains 4–6 cycles. Waking between cycles — during light sleep — leaves you clear-headed; waking from deep sleep causes grogginess. Restcalc works backward from your alarm (or forward from bedtime) to land you on a cycle boundary. Recommended total time in bed for adults is about 7–9 hours, following guidance such as the National Sleep Foundation's 2022 recommendations.

## Frequently Asked Questions

**How many sleep cycles do I need?**
Most adults feel best with 4–6 full cycles, or about 6–9 hours in bed. Five cycles (7.5 hours) is a common sweet spot.

**What is the 90-minute rule?**
Sleep cycles average about 90 minutes. Planning bed or wake times on these boundaries helps you surface during light sleep, reducing grogginess.

**How long does it take to fall asleep?**
Restcalc assumes about 15 minutes to fall asleep. Adjust mentally if you know you take longer.

**Is it better to wake during light sleep?**
Yes. Waking at the end of a cycle, in light sleep, is far easier than being jolted out of deep sleep.

**Can I use this for naps?**
Yes. A 20-minute power nap or a 90-minute full-cycle nap both avoid deep-sleep grogginess.

**Why do I feel tired after 8 hours?**
It may be timing, not duration. Waking mid-cycle can leave you groggy even after a long night. Try shifting your schedule 30–60 minutes.

## Science-Backed Sleep Tips

- Get 10–20 minutes of morning sunlight soon after waking to set your body clock.
- Keep the bedroom around 18°C (65°F) for deeper sleep.
- Dim screens and avoid blue light about an hour before bed.
- Keep a consistent wake time to stabilize your whole cycle.
- Avoid caffeine 8+ hours before bed — it lingers in your system.
- Use cycle timing (with Restcalc) to wake at a 90-minute boundary.

## Languages

- English (default, crawlable static content): https://restcalc.vercel.app/
- 中文 (Chinese): available via an in-page language toggle on every page. Note: the Chinese text is rendered client-side, so the English version is the primary crawlable content for search engines and AI crawlers.

## Monetization & Affiliate Disclosure

Restcalc participates in the Amazon Associates program. As an affiliate, the site may earn from qualifying purchases made through sponsored product links, at no extra cost to users. The site also displays Google AdSense ads. Affiliate and ad content do not affect tool results. See the Privacy Policy for details on cookies and data: https://restcalc.vercel.app/privacy.html

## Technical Notes

- Static site, no build step, no external runtime dependencies.
- Six independent tool pages plus a homepage; all calculations run client-side in JavaScript, and no user sleep data is uploaded.
- Structured data (JSON-LD): WebSite + ItemList on the homepage; WebPage + FAQPage on each tool page.
- Multilingual via an in-page toggle (English default, 中文 available).
